evidence-binding: n6-converge-shape 7118859cae1a
## n6-converge-shape — SETTLED design (repaired Candidate D) — DESIGN INPUTS for a FRESH build re-plan (PENDING, not shipped)

Two sequential build runs recommended (#636 then #630); each gets its own planner freeze. The 3 adversary repairs (R1/R2/R3) are folded in + independently re-verified (grep/read this node). The frozen n4 design would red all four chains as written.

### 0. Re-verified line-precise (this node)
- R1 #611 unconditional-mandate pin family CONFIRMED: tokens `on EVERY dispatch, tiered or not` + `the unconditional mandate applies identically to this dispatch mode` at validate-workflow-contracts.js:1026-1027 (+ byte mirror plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js:1026-1027), validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js:815-816, gitlab:804-805, gitea:809-810. Live INSIDE the Codex-dispatch block commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md:229,:236 (fenced paragraph :221-239).
- gitlab/gitea use ONE shared [command,SKILL] loop carrying #606 AND #611-fork together (worse than n3's "T14-only"): gitlab:774-814 — #606 teammate :793-794, #611-fork :804-806, + #602/#604/#605/#607/#611-join.
- File name is commands/workflow-next.md (NOT kaola-workflow-next.md).
- R3b gitea mr|pr) is a machine-pinned contract not drift: gitea:303-304 (command)+:342-343 (SKILL); gitlab pins too (:296,:335).
- R2 #624-fix finalize gate-flag pins to preserve: gitlab:369-373+:385-388, gitea:376-380+:392-395 (--resume-check/--gate-verify/--barrier-check/--verdict-check + workflow_path: adaptive).

### 1a. R1 — CORRECTED COMPLETE #636 Run-1 write-set
Two fences: FENCE A = remove `#### Teammate-Mode Dispatch` (Claude-live/Codex-dead) from the 3 SKILLs (pin-clean except #606 teammate tokens; the wait-budget/join content is in a SEPARATE `## Codex Join Protocol` section, NOT fenced). FENCE B = remove Codex effort-proof + v2/v1 dispatch paragraph (commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md:221-239, Codex-live/Claude-dead) from the 3 commands — carries BOTH T5b tokens AND #611-fork tokens. HAZARD (n1 splice): the always-live `- Instruct the role to:` list at :241+ MUST be preserved; only :221-239 removed.
Validator/test edits (n3's T5b/T14 relocation PLUS the #611-fork SKILL-only split n3 MISSED):
- test-route-reachability.js: T5b array :183-190 → SKILL-only (drop 3 command entries); T14 array :451-458 → command-only (drop 3 SKILL entries); T15 :475-482 UNTOUCHED.
- validate-workflow-contracts.js AND byte mirror plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js (identical edits, BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS): :957-961 (#582/T5b on command) DELETE; :983-994 (#606 all 6) shrink to command-only (drop SKILL :987-989); :1017-1029 (#611 all 6) shrink to SKILL-only (drop command :1018-1020) ← THE loop n3 missed, single largest correction.
- validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js (codex; owns github root command): :642-644 (#606 teammate on github SKILL) DELETE; :811-818 (#611-fork on [SKILL,root command]) shrink to SKILL-only (drop command :813); :827-830 (#611-join on root command: wait_budget/Writer kill-safety/writerHalt/delegation_outcome) UNCHANGED (in the command's Teammate-Mode block which STAYS on commands).
- gitlab-contracts.js shared loop :774-814 splits THREE ways: symmetric stay (#602 :778-780, #604 :784-787, #605 :790, #607 :799-800, #611-join :811-813); command-only NEW (#606 teammate :793-794); SKILL-only NEW (#611-fork :804-806). :818-819 (join A-F SKILL) + :827 (Writer kill-safety command) UNCHANGED. gitlab pins NO command-side T5b → no T5b delete here.
- gitea-contracts.js same three-way split: #606 :797-799 → command-only; #611-fork :807-811 → SKILL-only; symmetric stay; :823-824/:827 UNCHANGED. DO NOT touch :303-304/:342-343 (mr|pr contract, R3b).
- CHANGELOG.md.
Net corrected write-set = n3's map (T5b→SKILL-only, T14/#606→command-only) + ONE additional relocation per validator: #611-fork family → SKILL-only, realized as a three-way split of the gitlab/gitea shared loops. Exactly the gap the adversary proved reds all four chains.

### 1b. R2 — Layer-1 presence-check, specified so "by construction" holds
Layer 1 = manifest-driven, derived-universe presence checker, ADDITIVE-SUPERSET over existing T-pins (NOT bare replacement). Four requirements resolved:
1. CONTENT TOKENS not bare markers: each manifest entry = {block_id, topic, runtime_tag∈{claude-live,codex-live,both}, surface_type_tag∈{command,skill,both}, content_tokens:[distinctive substrings]}. Checker asserts every content_token appears on every derived surface. Hollowing a block (marker kept, prose gone) drops a content_token → red.
2. MIGRATION NO-WEAKER (additive-superset): ship manifest checker as NEW layer ALONGSIDE current T-pins; consolidate a T-pin into a manifest entry ONLY where a machine-checked SUPERSET PROOF confirms coverage. Superset proof = a test: for every assertIncludes token today (T5,T5b,T6..T15, the #624-fix gate flags + workflow_path:adaptive, #602/#604/#605/#606/#607/#611 families) assert it's a content_token in some entry whose surface set ⊇ the pin's. Any token not cleanly expressible STAYS as a residual additive T-pin (flip-premise 6). Net coverage provably ≥ today on the exact surfaces where #624 happened.
3. T3-STYLE STRUCTURAL RED-PROOF (modeled on test-route-reachability.js:104-119): a self-test over synthetic manifest+fixture surfaces asserting NONZERO exit for each planted defect (see §6).
4. FILE UNIVERSE DERIVES from emitted-targets registries (T1/T2 emittedSkillTargets/emittedCommandTargets, test-route-reachability.js:82-102) → new surface auto-obligated. Bidirectional orphan-sentinel: manifest (topic,surface) absent from registry → red; surface carrying a known block-marker with no manifest entry → red. Reverse needs machine-detectable markers — today only <!-- PIN/CARD --> blocks detectable; teammate-mode + codex-dispatch are token-only → ADD semantic markers `<!-- PIN: teammate-mode -->` + `<!-- PIN: codex-dispatch -->` (PROVENANCE_BAN-safe). Double duty: orphan-sentinel completeness + clean Layer-2 slot boundaries. RECOMMEND #636 adds these two markers as part of the fence (marker delimits exactly what the fence removes) so Run 2 inherits clean boundaries.
DECISION: Layer 1 is ADDITIVE-SUPERSET over the T-pins with a superset proof gating any consolidation — the only reading under which by-construction survives the "sentinel re-admits block-gone-yet-green" + "weaker-migration" attacks.

### 1c. R3 — drift-class corrected; gitea booby-trap removed
(a) Content-divergence is a real co-occurring class: #624 fix 4a84e791 fixed BOTH whole-block-absence (57-line adaptive prereq never propagated to 2 forge-codex finalize SKILLs — manifest catches) AND content-divergence-while-present ("three gates" above a four-gate block — manifest presence does NOT catch). ACCEPTED RESIDUAL: on the 6 finalize surfaces (Layer-1-only, hand-authored), present-but-wrong-content is uncaught FOR PROSE OUTSIDE the content-pinned token set. It IS caught for the tokens that matter most (four gate flags + workflow_path:adaptive + <!-- PIN: closure-audit --> + #285 verdict-block, all manifest content_tokens per R2-2). Residual materially narrower than "all content divergence"; Layer 1 is STRICTLY STRONGER than today's finalize regime (adds derived universe + orphan-sentinel). Closed only by Layer-2 finalize generation (flip-premise 2), DEFERRED (finalize is 2:1 rewrite 989:475 — force-rewriting a working surface violates precedence #1).
(b) gitea mr|pr) plank REMOVED — it's a deliberately machine-pinned contract (gitea:303-304,:342-343 canonical-pr-sink mirror), NOT drift. Run 2 must NOT carry the n4 "canonical must not reproduce this" plank; manifest/generator LEAVE these finalize pins untouched (finalize has no canonical under D anyway). If team ever wants gitea to drop mr| → separate deliberate contract change (flip-premise 7, default OFF).

### 2. Settled: repaired Candidate D (two-layer)
- LAYER 1 presence, all 18 (3 topics): single-source templates/routing/required-blocks.* manifest; derived-universe checker per R2. Layer 1 alone meets the literal #630 core AC on EVERY surface incl finalize; closes the whole-block-absent class that let #624 slip.
- LAYER 2 byte, plan-run + next (12 surfaces): canonical skeleton per topic + NEW render engine (frontmatter = two-shape surface-type slot [byte-identical frontmatters confirmed]; H1 forge-suffix token; runtime-conditional slots for teammate-mode/codex-dispatch; forge-noun rename TABLE not regex [resolve-agent-model.js un-renamed]; 2 sub-sentence splices via scoped token substitution incl Opus→non-lowerable-floor swap B2-purge requires anyway) glued to an edition-sync-style --check/--write byte-compare shell. Enforces #445 skeleton discipline; by-construction fences the runtime-dead blocks.
- FINALIZE = Layer 1 ONLY (manifest-guarded, hand-authored — NOT needle-only, NOT byte-generated). 2:1 rewrite not forced; R3a residual accepted.
- next: include in Layer 2 (its +2 SKILL-only sections are surface-type slots) if the engine is built; may start Layer-1-only + graduate. Planner's call at Run-2 freeze.

### 3. #636↔#630 COUPLING: ship #636 FIRST standalone, then #630 fresh re-plan on fenced base. Do NOT fold.
1. #636 is a PREREQUISITE not just consequence: a #630-generated fenced command without #636's validator relocation reds T5b AND the #611-fork command pins (proven). #636 removes the blocker.
2. #636 independently valuable + bounded (deferred #627 fix#2; corrected write-set fully mapped).
3. Both runs edit the four validators + byte mirror → "hidden shared surface" false-disjoint trap (conventions.md:263) → MUST serialize; within each run the 4-validator writes are ONE serialized frontier, never parallel legs.
4. #630 absorbs #636's relocation as manifest runtime tags (teammate-mode→claude-live/command; codex-dispatch→codex-live/skill) — refactor-into-manifest, not redo.

### 4. FULL corrected cross-edition write-set union (in §1a for Run 1). Run 2 (#630):
1. NEW scripts/<prose-generator>.js (render engine + --check/--write; canonical/COMMON, run from root, NOT forge-ported).
2. NEW templates/routing/{plan-run,next}.skeleton.* + slot data + forge-noun rename table.
3. NEW templates/routing/required-blocks.* (Layer-1 manifest).
4. GENERATED (@generated) 12 surfaces: commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md, commands/workflow-next.md (corrected name) + gitlab/gitea command twins + 3 plan-run SKILLs + 3 next SKILLs (verify skill dir names at build).
5. MODIFIED (manifest sentinels, hand-authored) 6 finalize surfaces — KEEP #624-fix gate-flag pins; do NOT touch gitea mr|pr) pins.
6. test-route-reachability.js — manifest-derived checker + superset proof + T3 red-proof + orphan-sentinel; absorbs #636 tags.
7. four validate-*-contracts.js + byte mirror — wire generator --check + manifest check.
8. package.json — wire <generator> --check into ALL FOUR chains + sync:routing (or fold into sync:editions).
9. docs/conventions.md, CHANGELOG.md.
#307 YES. opencode NO #307; generator emits no opencode surface — run test-opencode-edition.js only if skeleton's inline opencode mentions (:47-49,:184) change.

### 5. Build decomposition/roles/gates
Run 1 #636 (single coherent frontier): planner → 1 BUILDER (Sonnet) [corrected write-set; prose fences + validator splits tightly coupled — a fence without its validator edit reds + vice-versa → ONE builder not legs; the 4 validators are the false-disjoint hidden surface] → code-reviewer (FABLE) → adversarial-verifier (FABLE) → doc-updater → finalize (main-session-direct sink). Adversary charter: (a) no chain redded; (b) fenced blocks GONE from fenced surfaces; (c) command-side Teammate-Mode + SKILL-side Codex-dispatch/join-protocol PRESERVED; (d) all four chains green recorded sequentially. main-session-gate NOT needed (fully machine-checkable).
Run 2 #630 (fresh re-plan — planner re-derives): Node A (Layer-1 manifest + derived checker + superset proof + red-proof + orphan-sentinel); Node B (canonical skeletons + render engine + --check/--write shell); Node C (--write regen 12 → @generated + wire --check into all four chains); Node D (finalize reconciliation to manifest, Layer-1 only, keep #624-fix pins). SCHEDULING: A/C/D all write the four validators → treat 4-validator writes as ONE serialized frontier; do NOT co-open Layer-1 + Layer-2 validator edits as parallel legs; read/skeleton-analysis may fan out. code-reviewer (fable); adversarial-verifier (fable) with the by-construction red-proof battery. #307 YES. main-session-gate likely NOT needed (machine-checkable: red-proof battery green + four chains green + generated surfaces byte-stable under --write); retain only if planner judges manifest-replaces-T-pins a contract change warranting a value call (default off). Guards both runs: semantic markers only (PROVENANCE_BAN); B2 model-noun purge (Opus→floor scoped swap); forge-neutral CLI-token ban; #424 non-allowband .md write-set declaration.

### 6. By-construction regression test (the T3-style red-proof; deliverable 5)
Structural self-test over synthetic manifest+fixture surfaces asserting NONZERO exit for each planted defect: (1) block dropped from ONE derived surface → red (the #624 mode, empirically alive today); (2) block hollowed (content_token removed, marker kept) → red (defeats sentinel-re-admits, R2-1); (3) new surface auto-obligated (inject synthetic emittedTargets entry missing a block) → red (proves derived universe, R2-4); (4) orphan manifest entry (no surface) → red; (5) orphan surface block (marker, no entry) → red; (6) superset proof (every legacy T-pin token is a manifest content_token over ⊇ surface set) → fail on any dropped token (proves no-weaker, R2-2). Passing this battery = machine-checkable acceptance → main-session-gate unnecessary.

### 7. Flip-premises
1. Drift is content-divergence not block-absence → manifest insufficient → byte-gen mandatory all topics → forces finalize rewrite (toward A). Refined by R3a: content-divergence real but finalize gate-flag tokens already content-pinned; residual = present-but-wrong prose outside pinned tokens, accepted, closed only by Layer-2 finalize gen.
2. Team funds finalize info-architecture re-authoring (shared structured gate-list drives command Steps + SKILL Required-Steps) → finalize → Layer 2 → #630 uniform across 18 (closes R3a residual).
3. #445 skeleton drift judged tolerable → plan-run skips Layer 2 → #630 collapses to Layer-1-only everywhere (cheapest). n1's 3x #445 drift argues against for plan-run.
4. Standalone #636 blocked → fold into #630 as first sequential node (delays #627 fix#2, enlarges #630).
5. Paired .toml parity obligation discovered for any routing surface (n1+n5 found NONE) → generator needs md↔toml emission. Low prob.
6. (NEW, R2) superset proof reveals a legacy T-pin token not expressible as a content_token → stays a residual additive T-pin (superset-with-residual, not full replacement). Tolerance, not blocker.
7. (NEW, R3b) team decides gitea should drop vestigial mr| → separate deliberate contract change (gitea:303-304/:342-343 + gitea finalize surfaces). Default OFF; NOT part of #630 gen.

### Bottom line
Two-layer architecture (manifest presence all 18 incl finalize + scoped byte-gen confined to drift-proven plan-run/next; #636 first) is the settled shape. R1 corrects #636 write-set to include the #611-fork SKILL-only split (three-way split of gitlab/gitea shared loops + validate-workflow-contracts.js:1017-1029 shrink + byte mirror) — without it #636 reds all four chains. R2 makes Layer 1 an additive-superset content-token derived-universe red-proofed checker so by-construction holds. R3 demotes finalize content-divergence to accepted residual + deletes the gitea mr|pr booby-trap plank. Ship #636 first (single builder, code-reviewer+adversary fable, #307, no main-session-gate), then #630 fresh re-plan on the fenced base.

delegation_outcome: completed
